en.extract:
The long-billed crow (Corvus validus) is a crow that is endemic to the Northern Maluku Islands. This crow is large with glossy plumage, a large bill and white irises. It is classified by the International Union for Conservation of Nature as a "near-threatened species".


Description
The long-billed crow is a large crow growing from 45 to 53 cm (18 to 21 in) in length including its relatively short tail. The large bill tapers from a broad base and is black, as are the legs and feet. In adults, the plumage is glossy and entirely black; juveniles have dull black plumage. The iris is dark grey on juveniles, turning to pale blue to white in adults. The call of this crow has been likened to the yapping of a puppy "cruk ... cruk ... cruk".
The long-billed crow can be differentiated from other crows by its large size and glossy plumage, its long beak and its white iris. The only other crow within its range is the Torresian crow (Corvus orru) which has a much smaller beak and inhabits more open areas rather than forests.


Distribution and habitat
The long-billed crow is endemic to the Maluku Islands, an archipelago within Indonesia. It is a forest-dwelling bird and is mainly found on the islands of Morotai, Kayoa, Kasiruta, Bacan and Halmahera.


Status
The long-billed crow has a restricted range with a total area of occupancy of about 25,700 km2 (9,900 mi2). The forests in which it lives are being degraded and the population of the crow is estimated to be declining. However, it is a common bird and although it favours primary forest, it seems able to adapt to a certain extent to secondary forests, partly logged areas, coconut plantations and cultivated areas. The International Union for Conservation of Nature, for a long period of time, rated its conservation status as being of "least concern". On 2014 IUCN changed the status to "near threatened" because populations seemed to be declining more rapidly than was previously thought, but re-assigned it as least concern on 2024.


References

The '''long-billed crow''' ('''''Corvus validus''''') is a [[crow]] that is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the Northern [[Maluku Islands]]. This crow is large with glossy plumage, a large bill and white irises. It is classified by the [[International Union for Conservation of Nature]] as a "[[near-threatened species]]".

==Description==
The long-billed crow is a large crow growing from {{convert|45|to|53|cm|in|abbr=on}} in length including its relatively short tail. The large bill tapers from a broad base and is black, as are the legs and feet. In adults, the plumage is glossy and entirely black; juveniles have dull black plumage. The iris is dark grey on juveniles, turning to pale blue to white in adults. The call of this crow has been likened to the yapping of a puppy "cruk ... cruk ... cruk".<ref name="HBW">{{cite web |url=https://www.hbw.com/node/60777 |title=Long-billed Crow (''Corvus validus'') |author=Madge, S. |author2=Sharpe, C.J. |work=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive |publisher=Lynx Edicions, Barcelona |access-date=19 July 2016}}</ref>

The long-billed crow can be differentiated from other crows by its large size and glossy plumage, its long beak and its white iris.<ref name="HBW"/><ref>{{cite book|author1=Marzluff, John M.|author2=Angell, Tony|title=In the Company of Crows and Ravens |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Bc5YO5PnPmMC&pg=PT74 |year=2008 |publisher=Yale University Press |isbn=978-0-300-13526-8 |page=74}}</ref> The only other crow within its range is the [[Torresian crow]] (''Corvus orru'') which has a much smaller beak and inhabits more open areas rather than forests.<ref name=Madge>{{cite book|author=Madge, Steve|title=Crows and Jays|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_b-kAwAAQBAJ&pg=PA145 |year=2010 |publisher=A&C Black |isbn=978-1-4081-3527-3 |pages=145–}}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
The long-billed crow is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Maluku Islands]], an archipelago within Indonesia. It is a forest-dwelling bird and is mainly found on the islands of [[Morotai]], [[Kayoa]], [[Kasiruta]], [[Bacan Islands|Bacan]] and [[Halmahera]].<ref name="iucn 2024" />

==Status==
The long-billed crow has a restricted range with a total area of occupancy of about {{convert|25700|km2|mi2|-2|abbr=on}}. The forests in which it lives are being degraded and the population of the crow is estimated to be declining. However, it is a common bird and although it favours primary forest,<ref name="HBW"/> it seems able to adapt to a certain extent to secondary forests, partly logged areas, coconut plantations and cultivated areas. The [[International Union for Conservation of Nature]], for a long period of time, rated its conservation status as being of "[[Least-concern species|least concern]]". On 2014 IUCN changed the status to "[[Near-threatened species|near threatened]]" because populations seemed to be declining more rapidly than was previously thought,<ref name="iucn 2014">{{cite iucn | date=2014 | title=''Corvus validus'' | article-number=e.T22705963A61630954 | doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2014-2.RLTS.T22705963A61630954.en | access-date=12 September 2025}}</ref> but re-assigned it as least concern on 2024.<ref name="iucn 2024" />

zh.extract:
长嘴乌鸦（學名：Corvus validus），是鸦科鸦属的一种，为印度尼西亚马鲁古群岛北部的特有种。该物种的保护状况被國際自然保護聯盟评为「近危」。
长嘴乌鸦的栖息地为亚热带或热带的湿润低地林。
